access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q1.explanation: Câu 1 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 3 chứa căn cứ: "These early designs were fuelled by a central furnace, from which hot air was circulated throughout the building via a hypocaust — a series of brick pipes built into the walls and floors. However, these systems were only found within the homes of the affluent, as well as some public buildings such as temples. Later, hypocausts were also employed by the Ancient Romans to heat their public baths, where they were used to warm the sweat rooms.". Lựa chọn B (Greek system) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q1.evidence: These early designs were fuelled by a central furnace, from which hot air was circulated throughout the building via a hypocaust — a series of brick pipes built into the walls and floors. However, these systems were only found within the homes of the affluent, as well as some public buildings such as temples. Later, hypocausts were also employed by the Ancient Romans to heat their public baths, where they were used to warm the sweat rooms._

q2.explanation: Câu 2 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 4 chứa căn cứ: "The prototype included one of the first radiators, which was made of copper and was likely considerably smaller than modern radiators due to the high temperatures steam allows. This benefit was also one of the technology's drawbacks as it increases the chance of burning. Steam systems are considered more dangerous, complex and noisy than other types of central heating.". Lựa chọn D (Steam system) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.

q4.explanation: Câu 4 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 1 chứa căn cứ: "Central heating refers to any system which warms a building by circulating heated fluid — most often air, water or steam — through its rooms, usually via piping or ducts. Throughout history, most central heating systems have used a fundamentally similar principle. The technology has advanced over time and some modern systems are impressively sophisticated, but essentially still work in the same way.". Lựa chọn A (Egyptian system) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.

q6.explanation: Câu 6 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 5 chứa căn cứ: "Hydronic heating systems are probably the most common type of heating in modern homes. They heat water in a central boiler and pump it through metal pipes to the rest of the building. One of the first examples was used in 1710 to heat the Summer Palace in St Petersburg, Russia.". Lựa chọn E (Hydronic system) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q6.evidence: Hydronic heating systems are probably the most common type of heating in modern homes. They heat water in a central boiler and pump it through metal pipes to the rest of the building. One of the first examples was used in 1710 to heat the Summer Palace in St Petersburg, Russia._
